topic: money transfer to mom — Western Union vs Remitly

turns.content: yo whats better for sending money overseas. western union or remitly

Depends on where you're sending it and how the person on the other end picks it up. Remitly tends to have better exchange rates and lower fees for most countries. Western Union has more physical pickup locations worldwide. Where are you sending to?
sudan. khartoum specifically. ive been using western union but the fees are killing me bro

Yeah, Western Union fees can add up fast, especially for regular transfers. Remitly does serve Sudan and their fees are usually lower. The catch is the recipient might need a bank account or mobile wallet depending on the delivery option. Does the person you're sending to have access to a bank?
not really. my mom usually goes to the western union office to pick it up in cash. thats what she knows

That's a common situation. Remitly does offer cash pickup in some areas through partner locations, but the network won't be as big as Western Union's in Khartoum. It might be worth checking if there's a Remitly pickup spot near her before switching. You can look it up on their app.
yeah thats the thing i dont want to make it harder for her. she already has to go out to get it. if the remitly place is far its not worth it

Totally makes sense. Her convenience matters more than saving a few dollars on fees. Have you looked into WorldRemit? They're another option that sometimes has good coverage in East Africa with cash pickup. Could be worth comparing all three.
i havent tried that one. how do the fees compare

WorldRemit is usually somewhere between Western Union and Remitly on fees. Their exchange rates tend to be better than Western Union though. For Sudan specifically, they've had decent coverage. You could try sending a small amount through each one to see which gives the best rate and is easiest for your mom on the other end.
thats smart actually. ill try a small one on worldremit and see if she can pick it up easy. if its smooth ill switch

Good plan. And ask your mom what she thinks too — if the Western Union office is right near her and she's comfortable with it, sometimes the convenience is worth the extra fee. But if WorldRemit works just as easy, you'll save money every month.
yeah every dollar counts when youre sending regularly bro. those fees add up over a year
For sure. Even saving five or ten dollars per transfer adds up to over a hundred a year if you're sending monthly. That's real money. Worth doing the comparison.
yeah ill call her tonight and figure it out. appreciate it bro
No problem. Hope you find something that saves you a bit. Good luck with the test run.
